texto: I've spent my life wondering when I would earn the right to be a man again. Despite the undeserved good fortune of finding my true love, I always held a kernel of bitterness in my heart that things were not different... I will never be the man that I was. That man is dead—slain—for better or for worse, by my life as the Beast. In your words, the world does not need who I was.
explicacao: Citação de Jack Heckel sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “I've spent my life wondering when I would earn the right to be a man again.
